title: [InstCombine][FP] Fix nnan preservation for transform fcmp + sel => fmax/fmin

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions vectors but does not explicitly mention different floating-point types like double precision. The optimization applies to all FP types but double is not covered in the summary text.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define double @test_fcmp_oge_fadd_select_double_precision(double %in) {
#0:
  %cmp1 = fcmp oge double %in, 0.000000
  %add = fadd double %in, 1.000000
  %sel = select nnan nsz i1 %cmp1, double %add, double 1.000000
  ret double %sel
}
=>
define double @test_fcmp_oge_fadd_select_double_precision(double %in) {
#0:
  %sel = fmax nsz double %in, 0.000000
  %add = fadd nnan nsz double %sel, 1.000000
  ret double %add
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions preserving no-NaN from select arms but doesn't explicitly discuss the case where the FCmp instruction itself has nnan flag. The patch specifically propagates FCmp->hasNoNaNs() which is a distinct code path.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define float @test_fcmp_ogt_fcmp_nnan_flag_propagation(float %in) {
#0:
  %cmp1 = fcmp nnan ogt float %in, 0.000000
  %add = fadd float %in, 1.000000
  %sel = select nnan nsz i1 %cmp1, float %add, float 1.000000
  ret float %sel
}
=>
define float @test_fcmp_ogt_fcmp_nnan_flag_propagation(float %in) {
#0:
  %sel = fmax nnan nsz float %in, 0.000000
  %add = fadd nnan nsz float %sel, 1.000000
  ret float %add
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions vectors but all existing tests use float (f32) type. This tests double precision (f64) which the generalized pattern should handle but is not demonstrated in the known test dataset.
